{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Business Development Officer",
  "description": "Winock is a holdings company incorporated in Nigeria to provide an ecosystem of sustainable services to micro businesses through duly registered and licensed subsidiary companies. We are inspired to provide services to micro businesses because they contribute significantly to economic sustainability and social wealth creation.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	Winock Agro is seeking a results-driven Business Development Officer to accelerate our growth in the agro-commodity trading space.
	The ideal candidate will have proven experience in agro commodity trading within Abuja, Lagos, or Eastern Nigeria, and a strong ability to identify market opportunities, secure offtakers, and expand our commercial footprint across key regions.
	This role requires a hands-on professional who can drive revenue growth through strategic partnerships, door-to-door sales, and direct engagement with commercial customers such as hotels, restaurants, and other bulk buyers.


Key Responsibilities


	Identify and secure reliable offtakers for agro commodities across target markets.
	Develop and execute strategies to grow Winock Agro&rsquo;s market share in Abuja and expand into Lagos and Eastern Nigeria.
	Drive door-to-door sales efforts to onboard new customers and increase product penetration.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with commercial clients including hotels, restaurants, caterers, and food service businesses.
	Conduct market research to identify new business opportunities, pricing trends, and competitor activities.
	Negotiate contracts and close sales deals to achieve revenue and profit targets.
	Collaborate with internal teams to ensure efficient supply chain coordination and customer satisfaction.
	Provide regular reports on sales performance, pipeline, and market insights.


Requirements


	Proven experience in agro commodity trading, preferably within Abuja, Lagos, or Eastern Nigeria.
	Demonstrated success in sourcing and managing offtakers.
	Strong experience in door-to-door sales and direct business development.
	Existing network within commercial sectors (hotels, restaurants, food vendors, etc.).
	Ability to drive business expansion into new geographic markets.
	Excellent negotiation, communication, and interpersonal skills.
	Self-motivated, target-driven, and able to work independently.
	Good knowledge of the agro supply chain and market dynamics in Nigeria.


Salary


	N200,000 per month.
	Net plus 1% Commission.


Note: Please note that only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.",

Business Development Officer Job at Winock Limited




        
    

        
        Winock is a holdings company incorporated in Nigeria to provide an ecosystem of sustainable services to micro businesses through duly registered and licensed subsidiary companies. We are inspired to provide services to micro businesses because they contribute significantly to economic sustainability and social wealth creation.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Officer

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Winock Agro is seeking a results-driven Business Development Officer to accelerate our growth in the agro-commodity trading space.
	The ideal candidate will have proven experience in agro commodity trading within Abuja, Lagos, or Eastern Nigeria, and a strong ability to identify market opportunities, secure offtakers, and expand our commercial footprint across key regions.
	This role requires a hands-on professional who can drive revenue growth through strategic partnerships, door-to-door sales, and direct engagement with commercial customers such as hotels, restaurants, and other bulk buyers.


Key Responsibilities


	Identify and secure reliable offtakers for agro commodities across target markets.
	Develop and execute strategies to grow Winock Agro’s market share in Abuja and expand into Lagos and Eastern Nigeria.
	Drive door-to-door sales efforts to onboard new customers and increase product penetration.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with commercial clients including hotels, restaurants, caterers, and food service businesses.
	Conduct market research to identify new business opportunities, pricing trends, and competitor activities.
	Negotiate contracts and close sales deals to achieve revenue and profit targets.
	Collaborate with internal teams to ensure efficient supply chain coordination and customer satisfaction.
	Provide regular reports on sales performance, pipeline, and market insights.


Requirements


	Proven experience in agro commodity trading, preferably within Abuja, Lagos, or Eastern Nigeria.
	Demonstrated success in sourcing and managing offtakers.
	Strong experience in door-to-door sales and direct business development.
	Existing network within commercial sectors (hotels, restaurants, food vendors, etc.).
	Ability to drive business expansion into new geographic markets.
	Excellent negotiation, communication, and interpersonal skills.
	Self-motivated, target-driven, and able to work independently.
	Good knowledge of the agro supply chain and market dynamics in Nigeria.


Salary


	N200,000 per month.
	Net plus 1% Commission.


Note: Please note that only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Operations Manager",
  "description": "DropX is building an AI?assisted last?mile delivery network and marketplace (e-commerce infrastructure) in Lagos. Our unwavering commitment to innovation, quality, and excellence is reflected in every facet of our operations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operations Manager
Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Operations Manager will be responsible for turning strategic plans into day?to?day execution across hubs, riders, vendors, fulfillment, and the cold?chain.
	The ideal candidate will build and run DropX&rsquo;s operational engine, ensuring on?time delivery, customer satisfaction, and regulatory compliance, while tightly controlling costs and hitting growth targets.


Responsibilities
Operations Leadership:


	Oversee daily logistics operations for multiple hub locations, including order assignment, routing, dispatch, and delivery processes.
	Ensure smooth coordination between DropX&rsquo;s mobile apps (customer, rider, and vendor) and the operations backend.


Hub Management:


	Establish and manage local hub(s) where inventory (including temperature?sensitive goods) is received, stored, packed, and dispatched. Implement inventory controls, quality checks, and cold?chain protocols for refrigerated/frozen products.
	Ensure hubs meet safety and hygiene standards.


Rider &amp; Fleet Coordination:


	Recruit, train, and schedule delivery riders.
	Set performance standards (on?time delivery, customer feedback) and monitor rider utilization.
	Manage fleet assets or partnerships (motorbikes, vans, or third?party couriers) to meet demand.
	Address rider safety, incident reporting, and incentives.


Vendor &amp; Merchant Onboarding:


	Work with vendors (retailers, pharmacies, restaurants) to list products on DropX&rsquo;s marketplace.
	Ensure vendors meet onboarding criteria (licenses, product quality, and packaging).
	Train vendors on DropX processes (order fulfillment, stock updates, and returns).
	Foster strong vendor relationships to ensure high product availability and compliance.


Order Fulfillment:


	Streamline the end?to?end fulfillment process.
	Coordinate picking, packing, and shipping of orders from hubs to customers.
	Manage returns/refunds process and restocking.
	Implement measures to minimize shrinkage/spoilage (especially for cold?chain goods).


Customer Experience:


	Own the delivery aspect of customer satisfaction.
	Define service standards (delivery windows, communication protocols).
	Handle escalated customer service issues related to deliveries and logistics.
	Work with Customer Support to monitor feedback and implement improvements.


Safety &amp; Security:


	Enforce safety protocols for riders (helmets, PPE) and secure handling of cash on delivery.
	Ensure hubs have fire safety equipment and emergency plans.
	Maintain insurance for goods in transit and third?party liability.
	Oversee compliance with health guidelines in warehouses and deliveries.


Regulatory Compliance:


	Ensure operations adhere to local regulations (Lagos transport laws, electronic commerce guidelines, cold?chain standards).
	Maintain awareness of NERC/LASERC requirements (for any EV fleets or power usage), NDPR (data protection), CAMA (company law), and sector guidelines (e.g., pharmacy/medical delivery rules for RX).
	Coordinate with legal/finance on AML/KYC processes for payments and vendor vetting.


Data &amp; Metrics:


	Define and track key operations KPIs: (orders per day, on?time delivery rate, average delivery time, rider utilization, hub throughput, and fulfillment cost per order, cold?chain spoilage %).
	Implement dashboards for real?time monitoring and regular reporting.
	Use data to identify bottlenecks and drive continuous improvement.


Financial Management:


	Build and manage the operations budget.
	Control operational costs (warehousing, transport, staffing) to meet targets.
	Work with finance to set delivery pricing (70/20/10 revenue share model, as envisioned) and forecast revenue from logistics services.
	Provide input on investment needs (e.g., new hubs, vehicles, cold storage).


People Leadership:


	Recruit and mentor the operations team (hub supervisors, rider leads, vendor ops managers, data analysts).
	Set clear goals and accountability.
	Maintain a high-performance culture with regular performance reviews and training.


Technology Collaboration:


	Work closely with the CTO/Product team to implement technology solutions (route optimization, warehouse management, real?time tracking).
	Ensure operational processes are well?documented for the platform and that field feedback drives product improvements.
	Carry out other duties as assignedby management.


Qualifications and Skills


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Supply Chain / Logistics, Operations Management, Engineering, Business Administration, or a related field (advanced degree or MBA is an added advantage).
	5+ years in operations or logistics roles, including at least 3 years in a leadership position.
	Prior experience in last-mile delivery, e-commerce logistics, fast-moving consumer goods distribution, or related sectors is strongly preferred.
	Familiarity with cold-chain logistics (perishables or pharmaceuticals), pharmacy/pharma delivery (if RX is offered), and multi-hub distribution models.
	Proficiency with logistics and route?planning software, fleet management systems, and data analytics tools.
	Competent in Google Workspace or MS Office for reporting.
	Knowledge of Nigerian regulatory environment for logistics, eg: Lagos State transport/commerce regulations, NERC / LASERC


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their resume to:&nbsp;dropx.hr@dropx.africa&nbsp;and copy&nbsp;kola@dropx.africa&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",
  "datePosted": "2026-04-20",
  "validThrough": "2026-05-31",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "DropX"
    
  },
  "jobLocation": {
    "@type": "Place",

      "@type": "PostalAddress",

Operations Manager Job at DropX




        
    

        
        DropX is building an AI?assisted last?mile delivery network and marketplace (e-commerce infrastructure) in Lagos. Our unwavering commitment to innovation, quality, and excellence is reflected in every facet of our operations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operations Manager
Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Operations Manager will be responsible for turning strategic plans into day?to?day execution across hubs, riders, vendors, fulfillment, and the cold?chain.
	The ideal candidate will build and run DropX’s operational engine, ensuring on?time delivery, customer satisfaction, and regulatory compliance, while tightly controlling costs and hitting growth targets.


Responsibilities
Operations Leadership:


	Oversee daily logistics operations for multiple hub locations, including order assignment, routing, dispatch, and delivery processes.
	Ensure smooth coordination between DropX’s mobile apps (customer, rider, and vendor) and the operations backend.


Hub Management:


	Establish and manage local hub(s) where inventory (including temperature?sensitive goods) is received, stored, packed, and dispatched. Implement inventory controls, quality checks, and cold?chain protocols for refrigerated/frozen products.
	Ensure hubs meet safety and hygiene standards.


Rider & Fleet Coordination:


	Recruit, train, and schedule delivery riders.
	Set performance standards (on?time delivery, customer feedback) and monitor rider utilization.
	Manage fleet assets or partnerships (motorbikes, vans, or third?party couriers) to meet demand.
	Address rider safety, incident reporting, and incentives.


Vendor & Merchant Onboarding:


	Work with vendors (retailers, pharmacies, restaurants) to list products on DropX’s marketplace.
	Ensure vendors meet onboarding criteria (licenses, product quality, and packaging).
	Train vendors on DropX processes (order fulfillment, stock updates, and returns).
	Foster strong vendor relationships to ensure high product availability and compliance.


Order Fulfillment:


	Streamline the end?to?end fulfillment process.
	Coordinate picking, packing, and shipping of orders from hubs to customers.
	Manage returns/refunds process and restocking.
	Implement measures to minimize shrinkage/spoilage (especially for cold?chain goods).


Customer Experience:


	Own the delivery aspect of customer satisfaction.
	Define service standards (delivery windows, communication protocols).
	Handle escalated customer service issues related to deliveries and logistics.
	Work with Customer Support to monitor feedback and implement improvements.


Safety & Security:


	Enforce safety protocols for riders (helmets, PPE) and secure handling of cash on delivery.
	Ensure hubs have fire safety equipment and emergency plans.
	Maintain insurance for goods in transit and third?party liability.
	Oversee compliance with health guidelines in warehouses and deliveries.


Regulatory Compliance:


	Ensure operations adhere to local regulations (Lagos transport laws, electronic commerce guidelines, cold?chain standards).
	Maintain awareness of NERC/LASERC requirements (for any EV fleets or power usage), NDPR (data protection), CAMA (company law), and sector guidelines (e.g., pharmacy/medical delivery rules for RX).
	Coordinate with legal/finance on AML/KYC processes for payments and vendor vetting.


Data & Metrics:


	Define and track key operations KPIs: (orders per day, on?time delivery rate, average delivery time, rider utilization, hub throughput, and fulfillment cost per order, cold?chain spoilage %).
	Implement dashboards for real?time monitoring and regular reporting.
	Use data to identify bottlenecks and drive continuous improvement.


Financial Management:


	Build and manage the operations budget.
	Control operational costs (warehousing, transport, staffing) to meet targets.
	Work with finance to set delivery pricing (70/20/10 revenue share model, as envisioned) and forecast revenue from logistics services.
	Provide input on investment needs (e.g., new hubs, vehicles, cold storage).


People Leadership:


	Recruit and mentor the operations team (hub supervisors, rider leads, vendor ops managers, data analysts).
	Set clear goals and accountability.
	Maintain a high-performance culture with regular performance reviews and training.


Technology Collaboration:


	Work closely with the CTO/Product team to implement technology solutions (route optimization, warehouse management, real?time tracking).
	Ensure operational processes are well?documented for the platform and that field feedback drives product improvements.
	Carry out other duties as assignedby management.


Qualifications and Skills


	Bachelor’s Degree in Supply Chain / Logistics, Operations Management, Engineering, Business Administration, or a related field (advanced degree or MBA is an added advantage).
	5+ years in operations or logistics roles, including at least 3 years in a leadership position.
	Prior experience in last-mile delivery, e-commerce logistics, fast-moving consumer goods distribution, or related sectors is strongly preferred.
	Familiarity with cold-chain logistics (perishables or pharmaceuticals), pharmacy/pharma delivery (if RX is offered), and multi-hub distribution models.
	Proficiency with logistics and route?planning software, fleet management systems, and data analytics tools.
	Competent in Google Workspace or MS Office for reporting.
	Knowledge of Nigerian regulatory environment for logistics, eg: Lagos State transport/commerce regulations, NERC / LASERC


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their resume to: [email protected] and copy [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Business Development Executive",
  "description": "Dicalo Consulting Group is a management consulting firm specializing in a range of services, including business management and setup, accounting services, business registration, business consultancy, international tax advisory, and business plan preparation. At Dicalo Consulting, our business practices are guided by a set of core values that shape our approach to conducting business.

We are a group of dedicated professionals who prioritize honesty and unwavering transparency in our pricing and services. Our commitment is deeply rooted in strong ethical and moral principles, and we take great pride in delivering exceptional services. Even in the face of occasional challenges in the Tax, Accountancy and recruitment process, we maintain our steadfast commitment to providing services that not only add value but also bring gratification, consistently surpassing our clients&rsquo; expectations.

Management Consultancy &ndash; Our commitment is to consistently guide and assist our clients in adopting the most feasible and fitting business strategies for their unique needs and circumstances. We equally support our clients in preparing business plans so as to ensure that they are able to have an insight in the industry in which they are potentially seeking to do business in.

Additionally, we provide substantial support to our clients in the preparation of comprehensive business plans. This crucial step helps them gain valuable insights into the specific industries they are considering for their business endeavors.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Executive
Job Location:&nbsp;Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full Time

Job Purpose


	The Business Development Executive will be responsible for identifying new business opportunities, generating leads, promoting the company&rsquo;s fleet management and manpower outsourcing solutions, and building strong client relationships.
	The role focuses on driving revenue growth, achieving sales targets, and supporting the company&rsquo;s market expansion strategy.


Key Responsibilities


	Lead Generation &amp; Client Acquisition
	Sales &amp; Proposal Development
	Relationship Management
	Market Research &amp; Strategy
	Negotiation &amp; Deal Closure
	Qualifications &amp; Experience


Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Business Administration, Marketing, or related field.
	3&ndash;5 years of experience in business development or sales (fleet management, logistics, outsourcing, or B2B services preferred).
	Proven track record of achieving sales targets.
	Experience in proposal writing and client presentations.


Salary
N200,000 - N300,000 monthly

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should their CV&#39;s to:&nbsp;cynthiaasogwa@dicaloo.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Business Development Executive Job at Dicalo Consulting Group




        
    

        
        Dicalo Consulting Group is a management consulting firm specializing in a range of services, including business management and setup, accounting services, business registration, business consultancy, international tax advisory, and business plan preparation. At Dicalo Consulting, our business practices are guided by a set of core values that shape our approach to conducting business.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



We are a group of dedicated professionals who prioritize honesty and unwavering transparency in our pricing and services. Our commitment is deeply rooted in strong ethical and moral principles, and we take great pride in delivering exceptional services. Even in the face of occasional challenges in the Tax, Accountancy and recruitment process, we maintain our steadfast commitment to providing services that not only add value but also bring gratification, consistently surpassing our clients’ expectations.

Management Consultancy – Our commitment is to consistently guide and assist our clients in adopting the most feasible and fitting business strategies for their unique needs and circumstances. We equally support our clients in preparing business plans so as to ensure that they are able to have an insight in the industry in which they are potentially seeking to do business in.

Additionally, we provide substantial support to our clients in the preparation of comprehensive business plans. This crucial step helps them gain valuable insights into the specific industries they are considering for their business endeavors.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Executive
Job Location: Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full Time

Job Purpose


	The Business Development Executive will be responsible for identifying new business opportunities, generating leads, promoting the company’s fleet management and manpower outsourcing solutions, and building strong client relationships.
	The role focuses on driving revenue growth, achieving sales targets, and supporting the company’s market expansion strategy.


Key Responsibilities


	Lead Generation & Client Acquisition
	Sales & Proposal Development
	Relationship Management
	Market Research & Strategy
	Negotiation & Deal Closure
	Qualifications & Experience


Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, Marketing, or related field.
	3–5 years of experience in business development or sales (fleet management, logistics, outsourcing, or B2B services preferred).
	Proven track record of achieving sales targets.
	Experience in proposal writing and client presentations.


Salary
N200,000 - N300,000 monthly

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should their CV's to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Public Relation Specialist",
  "description": "OPPO, founded in 2004, is one of the world&rsquo;s leading innovators in smart devices and mobile technology. With operations in over 60 countries and regions, Dedicated to delivering cutting-edge innovation, premium user experiences, and stylish designs. OPPO continues to expand its footprint with over 290,000 points of sale and 1,900 official service centers &mdash; bringing the beauty of technology closer to people every day

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Public Relation Specialist

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Draft and distribute press releases in line with the brand communication strategy.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with key local media outlets to increase brand visibility and reputation.
	Maintain regular contact with media, KOLs, KOCs, and tech bloggers to coordinate timely and high-quality promotional content.
	Monitor and manage public opinion, especially during negative publicity or incidents, ensuring timely and effective crisis communication.
	Provide support to the broader marketing department as required.


Educational Qualifications &amp; Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Public Relations, Mass Communication, Marketing, or a related field.
	Minimum of 4 years of experience in public relations, media relations, or communications.
	Strong writing, editing, and content development skills.
	Established network with media professionals and influencers is an added advantage.
	Experience in crisis communication and public opinion management.
	Excellent interpersonal, communication, and negotiation skills.
	Ability to work under pressure and meet tight deadlines.


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 per month.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;cvpool2021@oppo-aed.ng&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Public Relation Specialist Job at OPPO




        
    

        
        OPPO, founded in 2004, is one of the world’s leading innovators in smart devices and mobile technology. With operations in over 60 countries and regions, Dedicated to delivering cutting-edge innovation, premium user experiences, and stylish designs. OPPO continues to expand its footprint with over 290,000 points of sale and 1,900 official service centers — bringing the beauty of technology closer to people every day

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Public Relation Specialist

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Draft and distribute press releases in line with the brand communication strategy.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with key local media outlets to increase brand visibility and reputation.
	Maintain regular contact with media, KOLs, KOCs, and tech bloggers to coordinate timely and high-quality promotional content.
	Monitor and manage public opinion, especially during negative publicity or incidents, ensuring timely and effective crisis communication.
	Provide support to the broader marketing department as required.


Educational Qualifications & Requirements


	Bachelor’s degree in Public Relations, Mass Communication, Marketing, or a related field.
	Minimum of 4 years of experience in public relations, media relations, or communications.
	Strong writing, editing, and content development skills.
	Established network with media professionals and influencers is an added advantage.
	Experience in crisis communication and public opinion management.
	Excellent interpersonal, communication, and negotiation skills.
	Ability to work under pressure and meet tight deadlines.


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 per month.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "description": "Paramount Web is a digital solutions company focused on helping businesses grow their online presence and achieve measurable results. The company specializes in website design and development, e-commerce solutions, search engine optimization (SEO), and digital marketing services.

With a strong emphasis on user experience, performance, and modern design, we build responsive, high-performing websites tailored to meet the unique needs of clients across various industries. The brand is committed to delivering innovative, scalable, and results-driven digital solutions that enhance visibility, engagement, and business growth.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Web Designer / Developer

Job Location:&nbsp;Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

What You&rsquo;ll Do


	Design and develop modern, responsive websites.
	Create visually appealing UI/UX experiences.
	Build and maintain WordPress and custom websites.
	Optimize websites for speed, SEO, and performance.
	Collaborate with marketing and content teams.
	Troubleshoot and improve existing websites.


What We&rsquo;re Looking For


	Strong skills in HTML, CSS, JavaScript.
	Experience with WordPress (themes &amp; plugins).
	Knowledge of UI/UX design principles.
	Familiarity with tools like Figma, Adobe, or similar.
	Basic understanding of SEO and web performance.
	Ability to work independently and meet deadlines.


Bonus Skills:


	Experience with React, PHP, or Laravel.
	E-commerce (WooCommerce, Shopify) experience.
	Graphic design skills.


Why Join Us?


	Competitive Salary (N100,000 - N250,000 monthly)&nbsp;and incentives.
	Work on exciting and diverse projects.
	Flexible working environment.
	Opportunity for growth and creativity.


Note:&nbsp;Are you passionate about building stunning, high-performing websites? Do you love turning ideas into interactive digital experiences? If yes, we want YOU on our team!",

Web Designer / Developer Job at Paramount Web Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        Paramount Web is a digital solutions company focused on helping businesses grow their online presence and achieve measurable results. The company specializes in website design and development, e-commerce solutions, search engine optimization (SEO), and digital marketing services.

With a strong emphasis on user experience, performance, and modern design, we build responsive, high-performing websites tailored to meet the unique needs of clients across various industries. The brand is committed to delivering innovative, scalable, and results-driven digital solutions that enhance visibility, engagement, and business growth.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Web Designer / Developer
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

What You’ll Do


	Design and develop modern, responsive websites.
	Create visually appealing UI/UX experiences.
	Build and maintain WordPress and custom websites.
	Optimize websites for speed, SEO, and performance.
	Collaborate with marketing and content teams.
	Troubleshoot and improve existing websites.


What We’re Looking For


	Strong skills in HTML, CSS, JavaScript.
	Experience with WordPress (themes & plugins).
	Knowledge of UI/UX design principles.
	Familiarity with tools like Figma, Adobe, or similar.
	Basic understanding of SEO and web performance.
	Ability to work independently and meet deadlines.


Bonus Skills:


	Experience with React, PHP, or Laravel.
	E-commerce (WooCommerce, Shopify) experience.
	Graphic design skills.


Why Join Us?


	Competitive Salary (N100,000 - N250,000 monthly) and incentives.
	Work on exciting and diverse projects.
	Flexible working environment.
	Opportunity for growth and creativity.


Note: Are you passionate about building stunning, high-performing websites? Do you love turning ideas into interactive digital experiences? If yes, we want YOU on our team!    

    

        Click here to Apply
